page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2 server family. It features 60 vCPUs mapped to 30 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 240 GB of dedicated system memory, providing an allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the c2-standard-60 demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 61473.27, placing it in the top 25% of tested configurations. It also shows strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth tests, reaching 226.55 GB/sec for read-only operations and 97.28 GB/sec for write operations. The processor's support for advanced instruction sets is highlighted by a strong Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 54838.03, utilizing AVX and AVX-512 vector extensions. Database operations also perform strongly, scoring 22356.86 in Passmark database tests. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth for small cached blocks remain in the average tier.

While the instance excels in parallel compute tasks and memory-intensive large-block transfers, it exhibits weaker performance in specific workloads such as text generation for small language models, which registers at 60.5 tokens/sec. Given its high core count, dedicated CPU allocation, and strong multi-core efficiency, the c2-standard-60 is suited for compute-bound workloads, high-performance computing, intensive database operations, and parallel compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor with 60 vCPUs and 240 GB of RAM. This x86_64 architecture server features dedicated CPU allocation and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61473.27, and strong large-block memory bandwidth. It also performs strongly in database operations and extended instruction tests. However, single-core metrics and small-model text generation speeds are average to weak. The c2-standard-60 is suited for compute-intensive workloads, parallel software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance with 60 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, designed for intensive database and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized server with 60 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 240 GB RAM, built for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring sixty dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and strong multi-core processing for demanding database and parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 240 GB of system memory
Strong multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth
Hardware support for AVX-512 and advanced vector instructions
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for parallel compilation, database operations, and compute-heavy tasks
